channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Amazon unveils Nova Act, an AI agent that can shop for you
item.description: Amazon on Monday launched its latest AI model, designed to take over a user’s web browser and perform simple tasks. The move places the e-commerce giant in more direct competition with artificial intelligence companies like OpenAI and Anthropic, which are also developing AI “agents.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: U.S. seeks to ramp up social media data collection. Here’s what to know about Homeland Security’s plans
item.description: U.S. immigration officials are asking the public and federal agencies to comment on a proposal to collect social media handles from people applying for benefits such as green cards or citizenship, to comply with an executive order from President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Apple’s Swift language gets version manager
item.description: Apple has introduced swiftly 1.0, a version manager for the Swift programming language that is intended to ease the process of installing, managing, and updating the user’s Swift tool chain. Swiftly 1.0 was announced March 28.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nearly half of businesses worldwide are embarrassed by their website
item.description: Despite spending big on marketing tech, many ecommerce businesses still find their websites embarrassing and ineffective.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AI and satellites help aid workers respond to Myanmar earthquake damage
item.description: Just after sunrise on Saturday, a satellite set its long-range camera on the city of Mandalay in Myanmar, not far from the epicenter of Friday’s 7.7 magnitude earthquake that devastated the Southeast Asian country’s second-largest city.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft Cancels Data Center Leases Amid Shifting Priorities - A Closer Look
item.description: Microsoft's decision to cancel data center leases has left many questioning the company's infrastructure priorities. We take a closer look at the latest developments and what they might mean for Microsoft's future in AI and cloud computing.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Using Privileged Access Management to protect Active Directory
item.description: Active Directory (AD) holds the enterprise’s crown jewels, granting privileges to users that determine what data they can access and what level of control they have over the IT environment. It’s such a vital system that when AD goes down, business operations often go with it.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Netflix reveals Black Mirror season 7 episode titles and there are some big clues for what fans of the sci-fi series can expect
item.description: Black Mirror has confirmed episode titles, including its first-ever sequel episode following on from USS Callister.
item.dc:creator: lucy.buglass@futurenet.com (Lucy Buglass), TechRadar

item.title: Scientists shielding farming from climate change need more public funding. But they're getting less
item.description: Public funding for agricultural research in the U.S. has been declining for the last two decades, a process Trump has rapidly accelerated by freezing or pausing support for a variety of research programs financed by the USDA, EPA and other organizations.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Retrocesos en DEI: la posición de los principales proveedores de tecnología
item.description: A raíz de la campaña de la Administración Trump contra las iniciativas de DEI (diversidad, equidad e inclusión), firmando órdenes ejecutivas que la prohíben en el gobierno federal, muchas empresas de la industria tecnológica se han alineado.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Private groups work to identify and report pro-Palestinian student protesters for possible deportation
item.description: Some groups pushing for deportations say their focus is on students whose actions go beyond marching in protests, to those taking over campus buildings and inciting violence against Jewish students.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Major dating app data breach may have exposed 1.5 million private user images online
item.description: Several apps from developer M.A.D Mobile had unprotected image servers that stored private and explicit photos without password protection.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: China's telecoms giant Huawei Technologies says its profit fell 28% while revenue surged in 2024
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Chinese telecoms gear giant Huawei said Monday its net profit fell 28% in 2024 while its revenue surged, as the company invested heavily in advanced technologies.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Businesses are struggling to scale AI across their business
item.description: Leaders and managers are more likely to use AI than workers because companies are only bothered about financial ROI.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: I tried Samsung's pricey new AI vacuum, and I now know there's no need for an AI-powered vacuum to exist
item.description: The Samsung Bespoke AI Jet Ultra is designed to be able to tell the difference between a mat and a carpet but why?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: GenAI, the future of fraud and why you may be an easy target
item.description: With the help of artificial intelligence, sophisticated fraud techniques emerging today are virtually undetectable to the untrained eye.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ECMAScript 2025 JavaScript standard takes shape
item.description: ECMAScript 2025, the next version of an ECMA International standard for JavaScript, will standardize new JavaScript capabilities ranging from JSON modules to import attributes, new Set methods, sync iterator helpers, and regular expression modifiers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk says he sold X to his AI company
item.description: Elon Musk on Friday evening announced he has sold his social media company, X, to xAI, his artificial intelligence company, in a deal that values X at $33 billion.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: These 5 new AI tools can help you do everything from managing tasks to improving your public speaking
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Hundreds of AI tools emerge every week. I’ve picked five new ones worth exploring.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: An AI-based drone that speeds up the detection and monitoring of wildfires is presented in Germany
item.description: EBERSWALDE, Germany (AP) — A company that specializes in early wildfire detection has developed a new, AI-based drone it says will help speed up the detection, location and monitoring of fires.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US, other G7 nations, fall behind in global tech race, study finds
item.description: The “Group of 7” (G7) nations are falling behind in key tech economic indicators such as high-tech exports, the number of software developers, and AI-related patent filings, endangering their future competitiveness, according to new study by London-based workforce consultancy SThree and the Center f
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Former Arista COO launches NextHop AI for customized networking infrastructure
item.description: A new player in the networking industry is tackling the unique challenges of AI infrastructure at hyperscale.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google Cloud partners with mLogica to offer mainframe modernization
item.description: Google Cloud has partnered with database and mainframe modernization software provider mLogica to help enterprises modernize their mainframe applications. The partnership will see Google Cloud combine its Dual Run service with mLogica’s LIBER*M automated code refactoring platform.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft abandons data center projects as OpenAI considers its own, hinting at a market shift
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly contemplating building its own data center as Microsoft backs off on its own infrastructure investments. OpenAI has privately discussed building and operating its first data center to house storage, which is essential for developing sophisticated AI models.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Judge allows newspaper copyright lawsuit against OpenAI to proceed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft lauds Hyperlight Wasm for WebAssembly workloads
item.description: Microsoft has unveiled Hyperlight Wasm, a virtual machine “micro-guest” that can run WebAssembly component workloads written in a multitude of languages including C and Python. Introduced March 26, Hyperlight Wasm serves as a Rust library crate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cyprus to fund desalination plants at hotels to reduce impact of water shortages on tourism
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Cyprus said Wednesday it plans to subsidize construction of private desalination plants at hotels to ensure the tourism-reliant island nation has enough fresh water too see it through busy summer seasons when the demands of millions of visitors put a severe strain on dwindling
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tiny electric vehicle takes half a parking space
item.description: Toyota's compact electric FT-Me is designed to tackle challenges of city living while offering an accessible and eco-friendly transportation solution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
